desc: Punchy, synthesized drum machine kicks paired with a snappy, high-frequency clap.
luna: API Vision Channel Strip (HPF at 30Hz, +3dB at 60Hz). 1176 Rev A (slow attack, fast release). Oxide Tape (subtle wow).
reaper: API Vision Channel Strip VST -> TDR Nova (dynamic EQ expanding 60Hz on kick hits) -> JS 1176.
notes: Nudge clap/snare exactly 2 to 5 milliseconds BEFORE the kick drum for a lazy, wide feel.

desc: The laid-back, Parliament-style live bass groove (e.g., 'Nuthin' but a 'G' Thang').
luna: 610-A Tube Preamp -> Kilohearts kHs Ladder Filter (slow envelope automation for a 'wah' swell) -> LA-2A.
reaper: Bass DI -> Kilohearts kHs Ladder Filter VST (automated cutoff) -> TDR Kotelnikov -> Pultec EQP-1A VST (boost 60Hz).
notes: Play behind the beat. Leave plenty of space between notes. Slide into the root notes.

desc: The iconic, high-pitched, gliding synthesizer whistle lead.
luna: Pure sine (Vital) -> Tube Delay (mix 25%, dark repeats). Lexicon 224 Plate (1.5s).
reaper: Vital (Sine). Mono/Legato ON, Portamento 100-150ms -> Tube Delay VST -> Lexicon 224 VST.
notes: Keep MIDI high (C5 or C6 octave). Monophonic melody only. No chords.

desc: Zapp & Roger style robotic vocal harmonies.
luna: Pultec MEQ-5 (surgically pull down 400Hz and 2kHz plastic resonances). LA-2A.
reaper: ReaEQ (Aggressive HPF 150Hz) -> JS Chorus -> ReaComp.
notes: Feed the talkbox with incredibly bright Sawtooth waves (filter wide open).

desc: A round, deep Moog-style sub bassline sitting under the live P-Funk bass for extra low-end weight.
luna: Minimoog -> Neve 1073 (boost 60Hz) -> LA-3A (slow glue).
reaper: Surge XT (analog bass patch) -> ReaEQ (boost 50-70Hz) -> TDR Kotelnikov.
notes: Keep it monophonic and locked to the kick's root note — it should reinforce, not compete with, the live bass.

desc: Laid-back, half-spoken ad-libs tucked low in the mix, classic Snoop/Warren G phrasing.
luna: Manley Preamp (warm, low gain). LA-2A (light, slow). Hitsville Chambers (short slap).
reaper: 1073 VST (low gain) -> ReaComp (2:1, slow) -> ReaVerbate (short room, low mix).
notes: Record close to the mic at conversational volume — don't push for loudness, push for laid-back cool.

desc: Dr. Dre's signature ultra-dry, cracking snare. Zero reverb, layered with a rim or clap.
luna: API Vision Channel Strip. HPF at 200Hz. Massive boost at 5kHz for the crack. SSL E-Channel Strip to flatten the transient.
reaper: JS Transient Controller (max out attack, cut sustain entirely). ReaEQ (+4dB at 5kHz) -> TDR Kotelnikov.
notes: The key is the dead silence after the hit. Use a noise gate with a 0ms release to physically chop the tail of the snare off.

desc: The Scott Storch signature. Plucked, staccato orchestral strings providing the main bounce.
luna: Neve 1073 (cut low-mids at 300Hz so it doesn't clash with the kick). LA-3A compressor. Hitsville Chambers (very short, dark).
reaper: HALion Sonic (Pizzicato Strings patch). ReaEQ (HPF 200Hz) -> ReaComp -> ReaVerbate (0.5s decay, dark).
notes: Keep the MIDI velocities extremely rigid and identical. Pan slightly off-center (L15 or R15) to leave the middle open.

desc: Ominous, heavily compressed grand piano (e.g., 'Still D.R.E.', 'Lose Yourself').
luna: API 2500 Compressor (Thrust set to LOUD, slow attack, 4:1 ratio). Pultec EQP-1A (+2dB at 100Hz).
reaper: Kontakt 8 (Grand Piano) -> JS 1176 (slow attack) -> ReaEQ (boost 100Hz and 4kHz).
notes: Play simple 2 or 3-note minor chords on the downbeat. Quantize perfectly to the grid.

desc: Eminem's incredibly tight, aggressive, multi-layered vocal technique.
luna: Lead Vox dead center (1176 Rev A into LA-2A). Double Track 1 panned L100. Double Track 2 panned R100. De-ess the doubles with TDR Nova.
reaper: Lead Vox -> 1073 VST -> fast ReaComp -> Kotelnikov. Left/Right doubles: steep HPF at 300Hz -> smash with 1176AE VST.
notes: Physically rap the verses 3 times flawlessly. Do NOT use a chorus or widener. Aggressively align the transients manually.

desc: Ominous, low orchestral hit accenting the downbeat, Dre/Storch-style tension builder.
luna: Kontakt 8 (Orchestral Hit) -> Manley Massive Passive (scoop mids for a hollow, tense tone) -> Hitsville Chambers.
reaper: Kontakt 8 -> ReaEQ (mid scoop) -> ReaVerbate (dark, medium decay).
notes: Reserve it for the biggest moment in the arrangement — a hook drop or a beat switch. Overusing it kills the impact.

desc: Dry, close-mic'd spoken-word skit dialogue between song sections, Aftermath-style.
luna: Manley Preamp (natural gain) -> LA-2A (very light). No reverb.
reaper: 1073 VST (clean) -> ReaComp (1.5:1, gentle) -> ReaEQ (light presence boost 3kHz).
notes: Keep it completely dry and upfront — the contrast with the reverb-heavy music around it is the effect.

desc: The 'Licensed to Ill' sound. TR-808s treated like a heavy metal drum kit.
luna: bx_blackdist2 (drive heavily). Pultec MEQ-5 (boost 60Hz and 3kHz for click). SSL G-Bus Compressor.
reaper: JS Distortion (Fuzz mode, low wet blend) -> ReaEQ (+5dB at 50Hz, +4dB at 4kHz) -> JS 1176.
notes: Let the 808 decay ring out slightly to create a droning low end beneath the rock samples.

desc: Massive, 80s rock-reverb snare hits.
luna: Send snare to Ocean Way Studios Deluxe (Large Plate). Insert Noise Gate AFTER the reverb. Fast threshold, instant release.
reaper: ReaVerbate (Large Plate, 3s decay) -> ReaGate (sidechained to dry snare, 150ms hold, 5ms release).
notes: The reverb should sound huge but get sucked into absolute silence before the next hi-hat hit.

desc: Slayer / AC/DC style guitar hits used as rhythmic stabs.
luna: Studer A800 (saturate heavily). API 2500 (crush the peaks). Pan hard left and right.
reaper: Sample -> JS Transient Controller (max attack) -> TDR Kotelnikov (brickwall limit).
notes: Truncate the guitar samples aggressively so they act as percussive hits, not melodic elements.

desc: Three guys screaming into one microphone.
luna: Neve 1073 (drive into red). 1176 Rev A (fastest attack/release, All Buttons In style).
reaper: JS Saturation -> JS 1176 (smashed) -> ReaEQ (High-pass at 200Hz to remove boominess from a group of guys).
notes: Record everyone simultaneously on one omni-directional mic to get natural room bleed and phasing.

inst: Guitars: NAM Arena Power Chord Wall
desc: A wide, distorted wall of layered power chords under the hook, using amp modeling instead of samples.
luna: NeuralAmpModeler (high-gain profile) -> NadIR (4x12 cab) -> double-track panned hard L/R.
reaper: NeuralAmpModeler VST3 -> NadIR VST3 -> ReaEQ (scoop 400-600Hz to avoid mud) -> TDR Kotelnikov.
notes: Track the same riff twice (not copy-paste) for a natural, slightly-different double that feels wide.

desc: A huge, cracking rimshot layered under the gated snare for extra transient snap.
luna: Trigger_2 (layer a sampled rimshot on snare hits). API Vision Channel Strip (boost 3-5kHz).
reaper: Trigger_2 VST3 -> ReaEQ (presence boost) -> JS Transient Controller (max attack).
notes: Blend it low under the main snare — it should thicken the crack, not become its own separate hit.

desc: Extremely dirty, muffled, slow-tempo drum breaks.
luna: Studer A800 (7.5 ips). Pultec HLF-3C (cut 10kHz to remove all shine). Fairchild 670 for glue.
reaper: JS Decimator (12-bit) -> ReaEQ (Low-pass at 6kHz) -> TDR Kotelnikov (slow release to make the break breathe).
notes: Layer a live, dusty funk breakbeat underneath programmed SP-1200 kick and snare hits.

desc: High-pitched, eerie, dissonant horns or sirens (e.g., 'Insane in the Brain').
luna: Pultec MEQ-5 (boost 2kHz aggressively). Tube Delay (short slapback, high distortion).
reaper: ReaEQ (High-pass at 500Hz, +6dB bell at 2.5kHz) -> Tape Cassette 2 VST (max noise/flutter) -> JS Delay.
notes: Pitch your horn/string sample up 1 or 2 octaves so it sounds thin, tense, and alien.

desc: A murky, heavily distorted upright bass loop.
luna: bx_blackdist2 (Triode-style setting). Kilohearts kHs Ladder Filter (Low pass at 400Hz to roll off the fuzz buzz).
reaper: JS Saturation (driven hard) -> ReaEQ (LPF at 300Hz, boost 80Hz).
notes: The bass should sound blown-out but completely muffled, taking up zero high-end space.

desc: B-Real style hyper-nasal, compressed delivery.
luna: Neve 1073 (High-pass at 250Hz, boost 1.5kHz). 1176 Rev A (smash 6dB of GR).
reaper: ReaEQ (Telephone filter: HPF 300Hz, LPF 4kHz, +3dB at 1kHz) -> JS 1176.
notes: Deliver the vocal from the back of the throat. Use extreme compression to keep every syllable audible.

desc: A hypnotic, droning Eastern string loop (sitar/tanpura-style) chopped and looped, the 'Insane' cousin move.
luna: Kontakt 8 (Ethnic/World library) -> Pultec MEQ-5 (boost 2-3kHz for the buzzy overtones) -> Capitol Chambers (short).
reaper: Kontakt 8 -> ReaEQ (presence boost) -> ReaVerbate (short room).
notes: Loop a 1-2 bar phrase exactly — the hypnotic repetition is the point, avoid variation.

desc: Multiple voices chanting a short, repetitive hook phrase together, stoner-rap crowd energy.
luna: 610-A Tube Preamp (drive warm). LA-2A (squash together as a group).
reaper: 1073 VST -> ReaComp (3:1) -> ReaEQ (HPF 200Hz to clean up group rumble).
notes: Record 3+ people chanting together in one take rather than stacking solo takes — the imperfect unison is the vibe.

desc: A collision of heavy funk breaks and 808s, mixed very loud.
luna: Drum Bus -> API 2500 (Medium attack, thrust LOUD). Pultec EQP-1A (boost 60Hz and 10kHz).
reaper: Drum Bus -> TDR Kotelnikov (peak crest control) -> Pultec EQP-1A VST -> JS Transient Controller.
notes: Layer tight funk breakbeats (James Brown style) directly over sustained 808 subs.

desc: Circus/calliope organs soaked in modulation and dirt.
luna: Organ (HALion Sonic) -> Brigade Chorus (max depth). Studer A800 (drive input to break up the organ).
reaper: HALion Sonic (Organ patch) -> JS Chorus -> JS Distortion (subtle overdrive) -> ReaVerbate.
notes: Play minor and diminished scales. Keep the phrasing carnival-esque (oom-pah rhythm).

desc: Heavy metal power chords fading in and out of the beat.
luna: NeuralAmpModeler (high-gain profile) -> Pultec MEQ-5 (pull out 200Hz mud so the 808 can breathe). Pan L/R.
reaper: NeuralAmpModeler VST3 (JCM800-style profile) -> NadIR (cab) -> ReaEQ (HPF 100Hz, LPF 8kHz).
notes: Don't play constant riffs. Use the guitars purely to accent the downbeat of the hook.

desc: Abrasive, screaming lead vocals layered with pitched-down demonic doubles.
luna: Lead: 1073 -> 1176 Rev A. Double: Pitch down 1 octave via Graillon 3. Run through bx_blackdist2. Blend underneath.
reaper: Lead: JS 1176 -> ReaEQ. Double: ReaPitch (Shift -12 semitones, Formant preserve OFF) -> JS Saturation.
notes: The pitched-down vocal should be mixed low, acting as a sub-harmonic shadow to the main lead.

desc: A dissonant, slightly-detuned bell/celeste melody adding an unsettling, off-kilter texture.
luna: Kontakt 8 (Bells/Celeste) detuned -10 to -20 cents -> Manley Massive Passive (thin the tone) -> Capitol Chambers.
reaper: Kontakt 8 -> ReaPitch (fine detune) -> ReaEQ (thin) -> ReaVerbate.
notes: Detune by ear so it sits 'almost right' — too far and it just sounds broken instead of eerie.

desc: A breathy, reverb-drenched whisper layer floating underneath the main lead vocal.
luna: Manley Preamp (very low gain, close mic). Ocean Way Studios Deluxe (long, dark plate).
reaper: 1073 VST (low gain) -> ReaEQ (HPF 300Hz) -> ReaVerbate (long, dark, high mix) -> automate volume very low.
notes: This layer should be almost subliminal — felt more than consciously heard.

desc: Bangladesh style. Incredibly sparse, hyper-punchy.
luna: API Vision Channel Strip. No drum bus glue. Treat elements individually. 808: bx_blackdist2. Snare: API 2500.
reaper: 808 -> JS Distortion. Snare -> JS Transient Controller (max attack). Keep mix completely dry.
notes: Zero reverb. Zero tape emulation. The silence between the hits is what creates the bounce.

desc: Using weird mouth noises or household sounds instead of hi-hats.
luna: Record tongue clicks -> Neve 1073 (boost 8kHz). Compress with LA-3A. Short Tube Delay slap.
reaper: Foley sample -> ReaEQ (HPF 500Hz) -> TDR Kotelnikov -> JS Delay.
notes: Quantize rigidly to the 16th note grid. Pan them dynamically left and right.

desc: A single, highly repetitive, abrasive synth note or whistle.
luna: Kilohearts kHs Ladder Filter (crank resonance). Tube Delay (sync to 8th note).
reaper: Vital (Square wave) -> ReaEQ (Bandpass 1kHz-3kHz) -> JS Ping Pong Delay.
notes: The melody should be no more than 2 or 3 notes repeating relentlessly over the 808.

desc: Constant background chanting and hypeman ad-libs panned wide.
luna: Group Ad-Libs -> 1176 Rev A (smash) -> Oxide Tape -> Send to 1/4 note Tube Delay.
reaper: Group Ad-Libs -> ReaComp -> JS Saturation -> ReaDelay (1/4 note, 40% feedback) -> Pan wide.
notes: Process the ad-libs drastically different from the lead vocal so they sit in their own space.

desc: Fast triplet hi-hat rolls giving the beat its rolling, bouncing forward momentum.
luna: SSL G-Bus Compressor (fast pump). Pultec EQP-1A (air boost at 10-12kHz).
reaper: ReaComp (fast attack/release) -> ReaEQ (+3dB shelf 10kHz).
notes: Program in triplet subdivisions, not straight 16ths — that's what gives the beat its bounce vs. a flat pattern.

desc: A short, punchy brass stab accenting the end of bars, Southern bounce horn-section energy.
luna: HALion Sonic (Brass Section) -> API Vision Channel Strip (fast compression) -> Capitol Chambers (short).
reaper: HALion Sonic -> ReaComp (fast) -> ReaVerbate (short room).
notes: Keep hits extremely short and staccato — one or two stabs per bar, never sustained.

desc: Blending live drum kit recordings with hard MPC drum machine hits.
luna: Live Drum Bus: Studer A800 -> LA-2A. Drum Machine Bus: API 2500. Blend them together.
reaper: Live Kit: IVGI2 -> TDR Kotelnikov. Drum Machine: JS Transient Controller. Group both -> ReaComp.
notes: Keep the live drums slightly loose and unquantized, but quantize the drum machine strictly.

desc: Thick, sustained, soulful basslines that weave around the kick.
luna: Minimoog -> Neve 1073 (boost 60Hz and 200Hz). LA-2A (slow, optical leveling).
reaper: Surge XT (Analog Bass) -> Pultec EQP-1A VST (boost 60Hz) -> TDR Kotelnikov (soft knee).
notes: Play actual bassline melodies (walking basslines), not just 808 sub hits on the downbeat.

desc: Curtis Mayfield-style R&B guitar licks and wah pedals.
luna: NeuralAmpModeler (clean Fender-style profile) -> Kilohearts kHs Ladder Filter (auto-wah automation) -> Brigade Chorus -> Ocean Way Studios Deluxe (plate).
reaper: NeuralAmpModeler VST3 (clean profile) -> Kilohearts kHs Ladder Filter VST -> JS Chorus -> ReaVerbate.
notes: Use the neck pickup. Play highly syncopated 16th note strumming patterns.

desc: Massive, soulful choir backing vocals layered heavily.
luna: Choir Bus -> Pultec EQP-1A (boost 400Hz for warmth, 10kHz for air). LA-2A (squash). Ocean Way Studios Deluxe (Plate, 3s).
reaper: Group Vocals -> ReaEQ (bump 300Hz) -> LA-2A VST -> ReaVerbate -> Kilohearts kHs Stereo (width).
notes: Stack at least 8 to 12 vocal takes. Pan them evenly across the entire stereo field.

desc: Sustained, warm Rhodes chords sitting under the whole track, classic Organized Noize warmth.
luna: Kontakt 8 (Rhodes) -> Neve 1073 (warm boost 200Hz) -> Studio D Chorus (subtle) -> Hitsville Chambers.
reaper: Kontakt 8 -> ReaEQ (warmth boost) -> JS Chorus -> ReaVerbate (short).
notes: Play slow-moving, sustained chords — this is a bed, not a lead. Let it breathe under the vocal.

desc: A single, impassioned, half-sung/half-shouted ad-lib phrase, Southern-church-influenced delivery.
luna: 610-A Tube Preamp (drive warm). 1176 Rev A (moderate). Hitsville Chambers (short slap).
reaper: 1073 VST -> ReaComp (3:1) -> ReaVerbate (short room).
notes: Deliver it once, full commitment, no punch-ins — the raw emotion is what sells this element.

desc: Premier / Pete Rock style gritty, dusty MPC60 knock. Relies on bit-crushing to emulate old samplers.
luna: Neve 1073 (drive input hard). Pultec EQP-1A: Boost and Attenuate the 100Hz band simultaneously to create a resonant bump.
reaper: JS Decimator (12-bit) -> Pultec EQP-1A VST (100Hz trick) -> JS Transient Controller (boost attack).
notes: Choke the hi-hats with the kick drum to simulate a sampler with limited polyphony.

desc: Raw, unquantized, multi-layered hi-hats that create the dragging, neck-snapping groove.
luna: Studer A800 (drop to 7.5 ips to roll off crisp high-end). Pultec HLF-3C (cut 5kHz).
reaper: JS Decimator -> ReaEQ (LPF at 9kHz). Use the MPC Swing script to push 16th notes late.
notes: Layer an acoustic hat with a synthetic shaker. Pan slightly apart. Vary velocities wildly.

desc: A warm, chopped jazz horn sample loop — the Premier/Pete Rock trumpet-and-sax move.
luna: Kontakt 8 (Brass/Horns) -> 610-A Tube Preamp (warm drive) -> Fairchild 670 (glue) -> Capitol Chambers.
reaper: Kontakt 8 -> ReaEQ (warmth boost 200-400Hz) -> ReaComp (gentle) -> ReaVerbate (short).
notes: Chop on the phrase, not the beat — preserve the horn player's natural breath and swing.

desc: A one or two-note sampled upright bass hit, doubling the kick's root for extra weight.
luna: 610-A Tube Preamp -> LA-2A (slow) -> Pultec EQP-1A (boost 80Hz).
reaper: ReaEQ (boost low end) -> ReaComp (2:1, slow) -> ReaVerbate (very short/off).
notes: One sustained note per bar is enough — this isn't a bassline, it's low-end reinforcement for the sample loop.

desc: Aggressive, in-your-face lyrical delivery with hard consonants, classic boom-bap battle energy.
luna: Neve 1073 (drive hot). 1176 Rev A (fast, aggressive, 8-10dB GR).
reaper: 1073 VST -> JS 1176 (smashed) -> ReaEQ (presence boost 3-4kHz for consonant clarity).
notes: Push the mic gain hotter than feels comfortable — a little grit at the source sells the aggression.

desc: Extremely loose, intentionally unquantized drum loops that feel human and drunk.
luna: Drum Bus -> Studer A800 (15ips). Pultec EQP-1A (Subtle smile curve: +1dB 60Hz, +1dB 10kHz).
reaper: Studer-style VST -> ReaEQ (Tame harsh 2kHz vinyl resonances). MPC Swing Script (57%).
notes: Turn off snap-to-grid. Manually nudge kicks slightly late, pull snares slightly early.

desc: The Madlib/DOOM trick. Pitching samples up on a turntable, then down in the sampler for alias grit.
luna: bx_blackdist2 (Triode-style). Pultec MEQ-5 (Boost 2kHz for that crunchy mid-range).
reaper: Pitch UP +12st. Render. Pitch DOWN -12st (no formants) -> JS Decimator.
notes: The resulting artifacts and crunch are the exact sound of 90s hardware samplers.

desc: A slightly-detuned, hazy Rhodes/keys sample loop with intentional pitch wobble, Dilla-esque warmth.
luna: Kontakt 8 (Rhodes) -> Oxide Tape (heavy wow/flutter) -> Manley Massive Passive (warm scoop).
reaper: Kontakt 8 -> CHOWTapeModel (wobble) -> ReaEQ (warm scoop).
notes: Automate a slow, subtle pitch drift over the loop rather than a fixed detune — it should feel alive, not static.

desc: A thick, slightly distorted sampled bass note reinforcing the low end under the chopped drums.
luna: bx_boom (short decay, tuned to root) -> Pultec EQP-1A (boost 60-80Hz).
reaper: bx_boom VST -> ReaEQ (boost low end) -> ParametricOD (light drive).
notes: Keep it simple — one or two sustained notes locked to the sample's key center.

desc: Extremely relaxed, behind-the-beat vocal delivery matching the loose, drunk drum swing.
luna: Manley Preamp (warm). LA-2A (slow, gentle).
reaper: 1073 VST -> ReaComp (2:1, slow attack) -> ReaEQ (light warmth boost).
notes: Track the vocal while listening to the swung drums on loop — the pocket has to be felt, not counted.

desc: El-P / Def Jux style. Aggressively gluing erratic, industrial percussion samples together.
luna: Drum Bus -> bx_blackdist2 (Triode-style setting) -> API 2500 (30ms attack, 3:1 ratio, 3dB GR).
reaper: Glitch Bus -> JS Saturation (barely clipping) -> TDR Kotelnikov (12dB peak crest control).
notes: Ghost stalk MIDI velocity: Kicks 110-122, Snares 100-115, Ghost notes erratic 12-28.

desc: Aesop / Blockhead weirdness. Off-kilter, metallic, glass-like melodies.
luna: Tube Delay (darkest repeats) -> Pultec MEQ-5 (scoop 1kHz-3kHz heavily).
reaper: Kontakt 8 (Glass/Mallet patch) -> ReaEQ (scoop 2kHz) -> ReaDelay (un-synced echo).
notes: Carve a 'hole' in the mid-range (2k-4k) to leave room for hyper-syllabic vocals.

desc: Hyper-intelligible, dense, fast-paced vocals that act as a rhythmic instrument (Aesop Rock).
luna: Neve 1073 (HPF 100Hz, cut 300Hz, +shelf 10kHz) -> fast 1176 Rev A (5dB GR) -> smooth LA-2A (3dB GR).
reaper: 1073 VST -> ReaComp (1ms attack, 6:1 ratio for peaks) -> TDR Kotelnikov (RMS leveling) -> ReaXcomp (De-Ess 5k-8k).
notes: Bone-dry dead center. If space is needed, use a 15ms micro-delay tucked underneath.

desc: Clanging, metallic, non-musical percussion samples layered into the beat for texture.
luna: Kilohearts kHs Ring Mod (metallic character) -> Kilohearts kHs Bitcrush (light grit) -> Pultec HLF-3C.
reaper: Kilohearts kHs Ring Mod VST -> Kilohearts kHs Bitcrush VST -> ReaEQ (shape).
notes: Layer these as ghost hits between the main drum pattern rather than replacing the kick/snare.

desc: A subtle, robotic-sounding pitched backing vocal supporting the hyper-syllabic lead.
luna: Graillon 3 (pitch shift + light formant lower). Manley Preamp.
reaper: Graillon 3 VST3 -> ReaEQ (HPF 300Hz) -> ReaComp (light).
notes: Keep the pitch shift small (3-5 semitones) — too extreme and it stops sounding intentional.

desc: Heavily processed beatboxing instead of drum machines.
luna: Neve 1073 preamp. Pultec MEQ-5: cut below 150Hz, +4dB at 4kHz to emphasize lip smacks. Compress heavily with 1176 Rev A.
reaper: Run through JS Decimator. ReaEQ (HPF 200Hz, +3dB 5kHz) and ReaComp with fast attack.
notes: Quantize exactly to the 16th note grid so it feels like a machine.

desc: The Neptunes 'Grindin' signature plucky, staccato synthesizer lines.
luna: Pure Sine wave (Vital) -> Kilohearts kHs Ladder Filter (fast envelope) -> Brigade Chorus -> short 20ms Tube Delay slapback.
reaper: Vital (Sine). ADSR: zero Attack, zero Sustain, incredibly fast Decay. JS Ping Pong Delay.
notes: Play completely staccato. No overlapping notes. The space creates the bounce.

desc: A vocoded, futuristic robotic hook line, Neptunes/Timbaland-style vocal processing.
luna: Graillon 3 (vocoder mode) -> API Vision Channel Strip (presence boost).
reaper: Graillon 3 VST3 -> ReaEQ (2-3kHz boost for intelligibility).
notes: Feed it a simple, catchy, monophonic phrase — the vocoder effect works best on short, punchy hooks.

desc: A resonant, squelchy synth bass with filter movement, a Neptunes/Timbaland low-end signature.
luna: Surge XT (saw bass) -> Kilohearts kHs Ladder Filter (resonant sweep automation).
reaper: Surge XT VST -> Kilohearts kHs Ladder Filter VST (automated cutoff) -> ReaEQ (boost 80Hz).
notes: Automate the filter cutoff per-note or per-phrase rather than a static setting — the movement is the character.

desc: Ultra-minimal, clicky, non-traditional percussion sounds replacing standard hi-hats.
luna: Kilohearts kHs Clipper (hard clip for click) -> Kilohearts kHs Transient Shaper (max attack).
reaper: Kilohearts kHs Clipper VST -> Kilohearts kHs Transient Shaper VST -> ReaEQ (HPF 300Hz).
notes: These should sound almost like mouse-clicks or typewriter keys — deliberately non-organic and futuristic.
